study_identification.study_title: A Phase 1B Study of Crizotinib in Combination with Pembrolizumab (MK-3475) in Patients with Untreated Advanced ALK-Translocated Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer
study_identification.indication: Previously untreated advanced ALK-positive non-squamous non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC)
study_identification.compound_class: Tyrosine kinase inhibitor (crizotinib) + monoclonal antibody anti-PD-1 (pembrolizumab)

study_design.type: Phase 1b, open-label, multicenter, multiple-dose, dose-finding, safety, and pharmacokinetic study in sequential cohorts
study_design.adaptive_details: Modified Toxicity Probability Interval (mTPI) design for dose de-escalation using an Up-and-Down matrix approach. Bayesian beta/binomial hierarchical model with target DLT rate pT=0.30, underdosing interval (0, 0.25), proper-dosing interval (0.25, 0.33), overdosing interval (0.33, 1.0).

treatment_arms.arm_name: Crizotinib 250 mg BID + Pembrolizumab 200 mg Q3W
treatment_arms.dose: Crizotinib 250 mg BID oral + Pembrolizumab 200 mg IV Q3 weeks
treatment_arms.regimen: Crizotinib BID continuously, Pembrolizumab 30-min IV infusion Day 1 of each 21-day cycle
treatment_arms._notes: Starting dose level (target dose). No plans to escalate above DL0.

treatment_arms.arm_name: Crizotinib 250 mg BID (3-wk lead-in) then + Pembrolizumab 200 mg Q3W
treatment_arms.dose: Crizotinib 250 mg BID oral (21-day lead-in monotherapy) then + Pembrolizumab 200 mg IV Q3W
treatment_arms.regimen: 21-day crizotinib monotherapy lead-in (Cycle -1), then combination as per DL0

treatment_arms.arm_name: Crizotinib 200 mg BID + Pembrolizumab 200 mg Q3W
treatment_arms.dose: Crizotinib 200 mg BID oral + Pembrolizumab 200 mg IV Q3 weeks
treatment_arms.regimen: Crizotinib BID continuously, Pembrolizumab 30-min IV infusion Day 1 of each 21-day cycle

treatment_arms.arm_name: Crizotinib 250 mg QD + Pembrolizumab 200 mg Q3W
treatment_arms.dose: Crizotinib 250 mg QD oral + Pembrolizumab 200 mg IV Q3 weeks
treatment_arms.regimen: Crizotinib QD continuously, Pembrolizumab 30-min IV infusion Day 1 of each 21-day cycle

study_design._notes_treatment_arms: Dose de-escalation design: starts at DL0 and may de-escalate to DL-1, DL-2, or DL-3 based on mTPI decisions. Up to 4 potential dose levels with crizotinib doses ranging from 250 mg BID down to 250 mg QD, and pembrolizumab fixed at 200 mg Q3W.

study_periods.description: Screening assessments within 28 days prior to study enrollment
Crizotinib 250 mg BID monotherapy for 21 days prior to start of pembrolizumab. Only applicable to Dose Level -1.
First 2 cycles of treatment during which DLTs are evaluated

study_periods.duration: Until disease progression, unacceptable toxicity, death, or consent withdrawal
study_periods.description: 21-day treatment cycles with crizotinib (oral, continuous) and pembrolizumab (IV, Day 1 of each cycle)
Review of AEs, concomitant medications; patients with ongoing treatment-related toxicity followed every 4 weeks

study_periods.duration: Until death or 18 months after last patient enrolled
study_periods.description: Survival status collected every 2 months; includes information on subsequent anticancer therapies

cohorts.description: Sequential cohorts of 3 patients per dose level using mTPI design to determine MTD. Approximately 30 DLT-evaluable patients expected. Completed when 10 DLT-evaluable patients treated at the highest dose with DLT rate <0.33.
Up to 40 additional patients treated at the MTD to confirm RP2D and explore safety, tolerability, and anti-tumor activity. Total of 50 patients at MTD (including 10 from Dose Finding Phase).
cohorts._notes: 50 total at MTD including 10 from Dose Finding Phase

intent_to_treat._notes: Not explicitly defined in this study. The SAP uses Safety Analysis Set and Response Evaluable as the primary populations.

per_protocol.definition: The per protocol analysis set (DLT-evaluable population) includes all patients enrolled in the Dose Finding Phase who are in the safety analysis population, and either experience DLT during the first 2 cycles, or complete the observation period for the first 2 cycles of treatment. Patients who do not receive at least 80% of the planned first 2 cycles of crizotinib dosing or both infusions of pembrolizumab within the DLT observation period due to reasons other than treatment-related AEs will not be evaluable for DLT.

safety.definition: The safety analysis set includes all enrolled patients who receive at least 1 dose of crizotinib or pembrolizumab. This is the primary population for all standard analyses and is also the primary population for the efficacy endpoints of PFS and OS.

pk_population.definition: PK concentration population: all patients in the safety analysis population who have at least 1 concentration of crizotinib, its metabolite PF-06260182, or pembrolizumab. PK parameter analysis population: all patients in the safety analysis population who have at least 1 of the PK parameters of crizotinib, its metabolite PF-06260182, or pembrolizumab.

other_populations.name: Response Evaluable Analysis Set
other_populations.definition: All patients in the safety analysis population who have an adequate baseline tumor assessment. At interim reporting, patients also need at least one post-baseline disease assessment (at least 6 weeks from first dose) or withdrawal/progression/death on study.
other_populations.purpose: Primary population for efficacy endpoints ORR, DR, and TTR

other_populations.name: PRO Analysis Set
other_populations.definition: All patients in the safety analysis population who complete the patient reported questionnaires for at least one of the items both at baseline and at least 1 post-baseline assessment.
other_populations.purpose: Primary population for patient-reported outcome analyses

primary.name: Dose-Limiting Toxicity (DLT)
primary.description: AEs meeting one of the definition criteria in Study Protocol Section 3.2 and occurring in the first 2 cycles of treatment (6 weeks) which are attributable to crizotinib, pembrolizumab or both. Severity graded according to NCI CTCAE v4.03.
primary.measurement: Occurrence of DLT during the first 2 cycles (6 weeks) of treatment

secondary.name: Adverse Events (AEs)
secondary.description: Adverse Events as characterized by type, frequency, severity (as graded by NCI CTCAE v.4.03), timing, seriousness and relationship to study treatments. Focus on treatment-emergent AEs (initial onset or increasing severity after first dose through 28 days after last crizotinib dose or 90 days after last pembrolizumab dose).
secondary.measurement: AE incidence, severity, relatedness by MedDRA PT and clustered terms

secondary.name: Objective Response Rate (ORR)
secondary.description: Percent of patients with confirmed complete response (CR) or confirmed partial response (PR) according to RECIST v1.1 relative to the response evaluable population. Confirmed responses persist on repeat imaging study at least 4 weeks after initial documentation of response.
secondary.measurement: Proportion of patients with confirmed CR or PR per RECIST v1.1
secondary.timepoint: Assessed at Week 9, then every 6 weeks

secondary.name: Duration of Response (DR)
secondary.description: Time from first documentation of objective tumor response (CR or PR) that is subsequently confirmed, to the first documentation of objective tumor progression or death on study due to any cause, whichever occurs first. DR (weeks) = [progression/death date - first date of OR +1]/7.02. Censoring rules identical to PFS.
secondary.measurement: Time from confirmed response to progression/death (weeks)
secondary.timepoint: From first confirmed response until progression

secondary.name: Time to Tumor Response (TTR)
secondary.description: Time from first dose of crizotinib or pembrolizumab to the first documentation of objective tumor response (CR or PR) that is subsequently confirmed. TTR (weeks) = [first date of Objective response (OR) - date of first dose +1]/7.02. Calculated only for patients with confirmed objective tumor response.
secondary.measurement: Time from first dose to first confirmed response (weeks)
secondary.timepoint: From first dose until confirmed response
secondary._notes: Summarized descriptively; not a time-to-event analysis

secondary.name: Progression-Free Survival (PFS)
secondary.description: Time from the date of the first dose of crizotinib or pembrolizumab to the date of the first documentation of objective tumor progression or death on-study due to any cause, whichever occurs first. PFS (months) = (first event date - first dose date +1)/30.44. Includes 6-, 12-, and 18-month PFS probabilities.
secondary.measurement: Time from first dose to progression or death (months)
secondary.timepoint: From first dose until progression or death

secondary.name: Overall Survival (OS)
secondary.description: Time from the first dose of crizotinib or pembrolizumab to the date of death due to any cause. OS (months) = [date of death - date of first dose +1]/30.44. Includes 12- and 18-month OS probabilities. Censored on last date known alive for patients still alive.
secondary.measurement: Time from first dose to death (months)

secondary.name: Laboratory Abnormalities
secondary.description: Laboratory abnormalities characterized by type, frequency, severity (as graded by NCI CTCAE v.4.03), and timing. Includes hematology, blood chemistry, coagulation, thyroid function, and liver function tests.
secondary.measurement: Worst toxicity grade per lab test, shift tables

secondary.name: Pharmacokinetic Parameters - Crizotinib
secondary.description: Cmax, Tmax, Ctrough, AUC0-8, AUCtau, and apparent plasma clearance (CL/F) for crizotinib. Cmax, Tmax, Ctrough, AUC0-8, AUCtau, MRAUCtau, and MRCmax for metabolite PF-06260182.
secondary.measurement: Non-compartmental PK parameter estimation from plasma concentrations
secondary.timepoint: Day 1 of Cycles 1, 2, 4, 6, 8; Day 15 of lead-in (DL-1); Cycle 6 Day 1 (full profile for DL-1)

secondary.name: Pharmacokinetic Parameters - Pembrolizumab
secondary.description: Ctrough for pembrolizumab. Population-based PK parameters if performed (separate report).
secondary.timepoint: Pre-dose and end of infusion at multiple visits

secondary.name: Patient-Reported Outcomes (EORTC QLQ-C30, QLQ-LC13)
secondary.description: Overall change from baseline in patient-reported lung cancer symptom, functioning, and global QOL scores per the validated cancer-specific EORTC QLQ-C30 and its lung cancer module QLQ-LC-13. Scores transformed to 0-100 scale. A 10-point change from baseline is considered clinically significant.
secondary.measurement: Change from baseline in QLQ-C30 and QLQ-LC13 subscale scores
secondary.timepoint: Cycle 1 Day 1, Cycle 3 Day 1, every 4 cycles thereafter, End of Treatment

secondary.name: Visual Symptoms (VSAQ-ALK)
secondary.description: Frequency of occurrence and degree of bothersomeness of visual disturbances as reported by the patients on the VSAQ-ALK questionnaire.

exploratory.name: PD-L1 Expression and Clinical Outcomes
exploratory.description: Association between tumor PD-L1 expression assessed by PD-L1 immunohistochemistry and endpoints of clinical outcome (response-related and time-to-event endpoints).

exploratory.name: Effect of Pembrolizumab on Crizotinib PK
exploratory.description: For DL-1: evaluation of the effect of pembrolizumab on crizotinib PK using steady-state AUCtau and Cmax comparing crizotinib alone (Cycle -1 Day 15) vs. in combination with pembrolizumab (Cycle 6 Day 1). Analyzed using mixed effect model with treatment as fixed effect and patient as random effect on log-transformed parameters.

safety_endpoints.name: Treatment-Emergent Adverse Events
safety_endpoints.description: AEs with initial onset or increasing in severity after the first dose of study treatment, through 28 calendar days after the last administration of crizotinib or 90 calendar days after the last administration of pembrolizumab, whichever is later, and before initiation of a new anti-cancer treatment.

safety_endpoints.name: Treatment-Related AEs
safety_endpoints.description: AEs judged by the Investigator to be at least possibly related to the study drugs (crizotinib and/or pembrolizumab), or for which relatedness is recorded as unknown.

safety_endpoints.name: Serious Adverse Events (SAEs)
safety_endpoints.description: All-causality and treatment-related SAEs, summarized similarly to treatment-emergent AEs.

safety_endpoints.name: ECG Parameters
safety_endpoints.description: QT intervals corrected for heart rate (QTcF default, QTcB). Categorical analysis of maximum increase from baseline in QTcF/QTcB (<30, 30-60, >=60 ms), maximum post-dose values (<450, 450-<480, 480-<500, >=500 ms), PR interval, and QRS complex changes. Shift tables for baseline vs. worst on-study QTcF and QTcB.

safety_endpoints.name: Vital Signs
safety_endpoints.description: Blood pressure, body weight, and pulse rate categorical analyses. Includes maximum change from baseline thresholds for SBP (>=40 mmHg), DBP (>=20 mmHg), body weight (>=10%), and pulse rate (<50 bpm, >120 bpm, change >=30 bpm).

safety_endpoints.name: ECOG Performance Status
safety_endpoints.description: Shift table of baseline vs. worst on-study ECOG performance status.

safety_endpoints.name: Deaths
safety_endpoints.description: Deaths summarized by two time periods: (1) within 28 days of last crizotinib dose and/or within 90 days of last pembrolizumab dose (on-treatment deaths), (2) beyond those windows. Cause of death listed.

primary_analysis.description: DLT occurrence during the Dose Finding Phase used to estimate the MTD via the mTPI method. The estimated MTD is the highest tested dose level with a DLT rate <0.33 in 10 DLT-evaluable patients. Bivariate isotonic regression (Dykstra-Robertson algorithm) used to smooth the resulting toxicity surface to a monotonically increasing one.
primary_analysis.statistical_method: Modified Toxicity Probability Interval (mTPI) - Bayesian beta/binomial hierarchical model with bivariate isotonic regression for MTD contour estimation

model_details.other_details: Target DLT rate pT=0.30; underdosing interval (0, 0.25); proper-dosing interval (0.25, 0.33); overdosing interval (0.33, 1.0). Unit probability mass (UPM) used for dose-finding decisions. Modification: if 4 DLTs at any dose level, that dose is unacceptable (DU). Re-escalation allowed if <=3/10 patients have DLTs.

hypothesis._notes: This is an estimation-focused design, not hypothesis testing. The emphasis of the final analyses will be on estimation of key summary statistics rather than hypothesis testing.

secondary_analyses.description: ORR summarized as percentage with exact 2-sided 95% CI based on the F-distribution. Best overall response (BOR) also summarized. Analysis based on Dose Expansion Phase including 10 patients from Dose Finding Phase treated at MTD.
secondary_analyses.statistical_method: Exact method based on F-distribution (95% CI)

secondary_analyses.description: DR analyzed by Kaplan-Meier method with median event time and 2-sided 95% CI. Calculated for the subgroup of patients with confirmed objective tumor response (CR or PR).
secondary_analyses.population: Subgroup of patients with OR from Response Evaluable population

secondary_analyses.description: TTR summarized with descriptive statistics (median and range) for the subgroup of patients with confirmed objective response.
secondary_analyses.population: Subgroup of patients with OR from Response Evaluable population

secondary_analyses.description: PFS analyzed by Kaplan-Meier method. Median event time and other quartiles with 2-sided 95% CI provided. Six-, 12-, and 18-month PFS probabilities summarized as product limit estimator with 2-sided 95% CI (calculated via normal approximation on log[-log(PFS)] then back-transformed).
secondary_analyses.statistical_method: Kaplan-Meier with product limit estimator for landmark probabilities

secondary_analyses.description: OS analyzed by Kaplan-Meier method. Median event time with 2-sided 95% CI. Twelve- and 18-month OS probabilities provided similarly to PFS.
secondary_analyses.statistical_method: Kaplan-Meier with product limit estimator for landmark probabilities

secondary_analyses.description: PK parameters for crizotinib and PF-06260182 listed and summarized by visit, dose level, and treatment (alone vs. combination). Descriptive statistics (n, mean, SD, %CV, median, min, max, geometric mean, 95% CI). For DL-1: formal comparison of crizotinib alone (Cycle -1 Day 15) vs. combination (Cycle 6 Day 1) using mixed-effect model on log-transformed AUCtau and Cmax with 90% CI for geometric mean ratio.
secondary_analyses.statistical_method: Descriptive statistics; Mixed-effect model (for DL-1 drug interaction assessment)

secondary_analyses.description: Summary statistics (mean, SD, median, range, 95% CI) of actual scores and change from baseline at each time point for all EORTC QLQ-C30 and QLQ-LC13 subscales. Patients classified as improved, stable, or deteriorated based on 10-point change threshold. Visit windows applied per Appendix 5.
secondary_analyses.statistical_method: Descriptive statistics; categorical responder analysis (10-point threshold)

subgroup_analyses.method: All safety and efficacy summaries presented by dose level and/or by dose level within dosing regimen

subgroup_analyses.subgroup_variable: Ethnicity and race group (Asians vs. Non-Asians)
subgroup_analyses.method: PK Ctrough,ss summaries by ethnicity and race group

interim_analyses._notes: This is an open-label, single-arm trial for which no formal interim analysis is planned. The final analysis of efficacy will be performed approximately 18 months after enrollment of the last patient; however, earlier analyses may be performed for publication and regulatory reporting purposes.

missing_data.primary_strategy: No imputation for efficacy analyses. Time-to-event endpoints use censoring; for ORR, patients with no post-baseline tumor evaluations are counted as non-responders.

imputation_methods.details: For start dates: missing day replaced by 1st of month, missing day+month replaced by Jan 1. For stop dates: missing day replaced by last day of month, missing day+month replaced by Dec 31. Exception: if resulting in negative duration, set to 0. For PFS, TTR, DR, and OS, negative durations reset to 1 day.
Patients without events censored at last evaluable tumor assessment (PFS/DR) or last date known alive (OS). Patients with inadequate baseline assessments censored at date of first dose.
Patients who do not have on-study radiographic tumor re-evaluation or who die, progress, or drop out prior to reaching CR or PR will be counted as non-responders.
Concentrations below the limit of quantification (BLQ) set to zero for calculations. BLQ excluded from geometric mean calculations.

If multiple answers given, more severe answer counted. Multi-item subscale considered missing if less than half of constituent items answered. Single-item subscales missing if item incomplete. Scoring per EORTC manual.

estimand_framework._notes: Estimand framework (ICH E9 R1) not explicitly referenced. Protocol predates widespread adoption.

baseline_definition.general_rule: Last observation within 28 days prior to the first dose of study treatment

exceptions.rule: For DL-1, baseline assessment will be the one obtained prior to start of crizotinib
For DL-1, baseline ECG assessment will be the one obtained prior to start of crizotinib during the crizotinib lead-in period

Baseline tumor evaluations must be performed within 4 weeks (28 days) prior to the first dose of study treatment; presence of at least one measurable lesion per RECIST v1.1; all lesions recorded at baseline must have an associated status on the CRF

assessments: Labs (hematology, blood chemistry, coagulation, thyroid, urinalysis, pregnancy)
CT/MRI scan (chest, abdomen, pelvis; brain if indicated; bone scan Q12W if indicated)
visits._notes: First tumor assessment at Week 9 (+7 days) after start of pembrolizumab, then every 6 weeks (+/-7 days) thereafter
At least 28 days but no more than 35 days after last dose of study treatment
Until death or 18 months after enrollment of last patient

visit_schedule.windowing_rules: All patient visits can be performed +/-2 days of scheduled visit. One cycle = 21 days. Visit windows for PRO endpoints defined in SAP Appendix 5: Cycle 1 Day 1 (<=Day 1), Cycle 2 (Days 12-32, target Day 22), Cycle 3 (Days 33-53, target Day 43), etc. with 21-day windows.

per_arm._notes: Not a parallel-arm study. Dose Finding Phase ~30 DLT-evaluable patients across dose levels; Dose Expansion Phase 50 total patients at MTD (including 10 from Dose Finding Phase)

sample_size.assumptions: Sample size based on logistic feasibility and past experience with Phase 1b studies in Oncology, not entirely driven by statistical considerations. Dose Expansion Phase sample size of 50 patients calculated using 1-sample survival method to test 18-month PFS probability >=50% vs. <=32% (historical control from crizotinib arm of Study A8081014).
sample_size.method: 1-sample survival method (for Dose Expansion Phase); mTPI Bayesian design (for Dose Finding Phase)

planned_tlg_list._notes: The SAP does not include an explicit numbered TLG list or mock shells. The following are derived from the planned analyses described in SAP Sections 8.3.1-8.3.5 and 8.4.

tables.title: Patient Disposition
tables.description: Study conduct and patient disposition; reasons for discontinuation

tables.title: Baseline Demographics and Characteristics
tables.description: Age, gender, height, weight, ethnicity, ECOG PS, smoking history, histopathological classification, stage at diagnosis, extent of disease, medical history

tables.title: Treatment Administration/Compliance
tables.description: Total cycles, median cycles, dose intensity, dose modifications, dose interruptions, dose delays by medication

tables.title: DLT Summary by Dose Level
tables.description: Number and rate of DLTs at each dose level

tables.title: Overall Summary of Treatment-Emergent AEs
tables.description: Number and % patients with any AE, any SAE, any treatment-related AE, any treatment-related SAE, discontinuation/dose reduction due to AE

tables.title: Treatment-Emergent AEs by MedDRA PT and Maximum CTCAE Grade
tables.description: All-causality TEAEs by preferred term/clustered term and maximum grade, in decreasing order of frequency, by dose level

tables.title: Treatment-Emergent AEs by Grade Group (1-2, 3-4, 5)
tables.description: AEs by preferred term/clustered term and maximum CTCAE grade group, decreasing frequency

tables.title: Treatment-Related AEs by MedDRA PT and Maximum CTCAE Grade
tables.description: Treatment-related TEAEs similarly to T-6

tables.title: Treatment-Emergent SAEs
tables.description: All-causality and treatment-related SAEs

tables.title: TEAEs Leading to Permanent/Temporary Discontinuation or Dose Reduction
tables.description: AEs associated with treatment modifications

tables.title: AE Summary by Cycle (Cycle 1, Cycle 2, Cycles >=3)
tables.description: AEs presented by cycle period; for DL-1 includes lead-in period

tables.title: Deaths Summary
tables.description: Deaths by time period (on-treatment vs. post-treatment) and cause of death

tables.title: Laboratory Test Abnormalities by Worst CTCAE Grade
tables.description: Hematology and blood chemistry by worst grade; by cycle (Cycle 1, Cycle 2, Cycles >=3)

tables.title: Laboratory Shift Tables (Baseline vs. Maximum Post-Baseline CTCAE Grade)
tables.description: Shift summary of baseline grade by maximum post-baseline CTCAE grade

tables.title: E-DISH Scatter Plots (ALT/AST vs. Total Bilirubin)
tables.description: Evaluation of Drug-Induced Serious Hepatotoxicity

tables.title: ECG Parameters Summary
tables.description: HR, RR, PR, QRS, QTcF, QTcB by dose level; categorical analysis of QTc changes and outliers

tables.title: ECG Shift Tables (Baseline vs. Worst On-Study QTcF/QTcB)
tables.description: Shift tables using maximum CTCAE Grade

tables.title: Vital Signs Categorical Analysis
tables.description: Blood pressure, body weight, pulse rate categorical thresholds

tables.title: Vital Signs Descriptive Statistics by Visit
tables.description: Baseline and change from baseline in BP and pulse rate by visit

tables.title: ECOG Performance Status Shift Table
tables.description: Baseline vs. worst on-study status

tables.title: Best Overall Response Summary
tables.description: BOR categories (CR, PR, SD, PD, Early Death, IND) and ORR with 95% CI

tables.title: PFS Summary (Kaplan-Meier)
tables.description: Median PFS, quartiles, 6-, 12-, 18-month PFS probabilities with 95% CIs

tables.title: OS Summary (Kaplan-Meier)
tables.description: Median OS, 12- and 18-month OS probabilities with 95% CIs

tables.title: Duration of Response Summary (Kaplan-Meier)
tables.description: Median DR with 95% CI for confirmed responders

tables.title: Time to Response Summary
tables.description: Median and range of TTR for confirmed responders

tables.title: Crizotinib Plasma Concentrations by Visit and Dose Level
tables.description: Descriptive statistics and geometric means with 95% CI

tables.title: PF-06260182 Plasma Concentrations by Visit and Dose Level
tables.description: Descriptive statistics for metabolite concentrations

tables.title: Pembrolizumab Serum Concentrations by Visit and Dose Level
tables.description: Descriptive statistics for pembrolizumab trough concentrations

tables.title: Crizotinib and PF-06260182 PK Parameters (DL-1)
tables.description: Cmax, Tmax, Ctrough, AUC0-8, AUCtau, CL/F for crizotinib; Cmax, Tmax, Ctrough, AUC0-8, AUCtau, MRAUCtau, MRCmax for PF-06260182, by visit and treatment

tables.title: Effect of Pembrolizumab on Crizotinib PK (Mixed-Effect Model, DL-1)
tables.description: 90% CI for geometric mean ratio (combination/alone) of AUCtau and Cmax

tables.title: Crizotinib Steady-State Ctrough Summary by Visit and Ethnicity
tables.description: Ctrough,ss and Ctrough,ss,mean by visit, ethnicity and race group

tables.title: PRO Instrument Compliance Rates
tables.description: Number and % completing QLQ-C30, QLQ-LC13, VSAQ-ALK at each time point

tables.title: EORTC QLQ-C30 and QLQ-LC13 Scores and Change from Baseline
tables.description: Mean, SD, median, range, 95% CI of actual scores and change from baseline at each time point

tables.title: PRO Responder Analysis (Improved/Stable/Deteriorated)
tables.description: Number and % of patients improved, worsened, or stable (10-point threshold) for each domain

tables.title: VSAQ-ALK Frequency Analysis
tables.description: Frequency of occurrence and degree of bothersomeness of visual disturbances per item

tables.title: Prior and Concomitant Medications Summary
tables.description: Number of patients with concomitant drug/non-drug treatments

listings.title: DLT Listing by Dose Level
listings.description: Patient ID, DLT term, date, time from treatment start to onset, time to resolution, action taken

listings.title: Patients with Grade >=3 Laboratory Toxicities
listings.description: Listing of patients who developed grade >=3 lab toxicities

listings.title: Efficacy Listings (Dose Finding Phase)
listings.description: Individual patient efficacy data listings for the Dose Finding Phase

listings.title: Other Laboratory Test Results
listings.description: Individual patient test results for labs without CTCAE definitions

listings.title: Prior and Follow-up Systemic Therapy Listing
listings.description: Prior and follow-up systemic therapy for primary diagnosis

listings.title: Prior and Concomitant Drug/Non-Drug Treatment Listings
listings.description: Separate listings for drug and non-drug treatments

listings.title: Protocol Deviations Listing
listings.description: Full list of protocol deviations related to statistical analyses or analysis populations

figures.title: Kaplan-Meier Plot of DR
figures.description: Kaplan-Meier curve for Duration of Response

figures.title: Crizotinib Mean/Median Concentration-Time Plots
figures.description: By dose level, visit, on linear-linear and log-linear scales

figures.title: Crizotinib Individual Concentration-Time Profiles
figures.description: By treatment, dose level, cycle, and day

figures.title: Ctrough Plots by Visit (Crizotinib and PF-06260182)
figures.description: Median/mean Ctrough,ss by visit, ethnicity, and race group (linear plots)

figures.title: Pembrolizumab Trough Concentration Plots
figures.description: Box-whisker or linear plots by visit to assess steady-state attainment

figures.title: Box Plots for AUCtau and Cmax (Crizotinib and PF-06260182)
figures.description: Comparison of crizotinib alone vs. combination (DL-1) with geometric mean and median overlaid

figures.title: E-DISH Scatter Plots

figures.title: AE Duration Kaplan-Meier Plots
figures.description: Duration of AEs of special interest, if applicable

fields_missing: DLT definition criteria details (referenced as Protocol Section 3.2, content partially redacted/CCI in both documents)
Some exploratory objectives redacted (CCI blocks in both Protocol and SAP)
Section 5.6 of SAP (population between PRO and Treatment Misallocations) redacted
Some biomarker/immunogenicity analysis details redacted (CCI blocks)
inconsistencies_found: No material inconsistencies found between Protocol and SAP. SAP text taken directly from the protocol is presented in italics and matches faithfully.
reviewer_attention_items: DLT definition criteria: The specific DLT definition criteria are in Protocol Section 3.2, which was not included in the pages read. The SAP references it but does not reproduce the full criteria. A reviewer should verify the complete DLT definition.

Redacted (CCI) sections: Multiple sections in both documents are redacted as Confidential Commercial Information. These may contain additional exploratory objectives, biomarker endpoints, and analysis details that could expand the TLG list.

Dose levels table (Table 5/6 in Protocol): The specific crizotinib dose amounts for each dose level are referenced but the detailed table was not fully visible in the pages read. The 4 dose levels (DL0, DL-1, DL-2, DL-3) are confirmed from SAP text.
No formal TLG list or mock shells in SAP: The planned TLGs were derived from the analysis descriptions. A reviewer should confirm completeness.
recommendations: Obtain the complete DLT definition criteria from Protocol Section 3.2 to ensure the primary endpoint is fully captured.
Review CCI-redacted sections if access is available, as they may contain additional endpoints and analyses.

The derived TLG list (~36 tables, 8 listings, 11 figures = ~55 total) is appropriate for a Phase 1b oncology study. Consider whether additional AE-of-special-interest tables (e.g., immune-related AEs for pembrolizumab) are needed.
Confirm that the complete dose level table (DL0 through DL-3) matches the extracted arm definitions before proceeding to TLG shell generation.
